sections.content: Under SOLAS regulations, ships fitted with VHF radio must maintain a continuous listening watch on Channel 16 (156.800 MHz) and DSC Channel 70. The dual-watch feature allows monitoring Channel 16 while working on another channel. All radio activity should be logged in the ship's radio log.

rows: Continuous while at sea (automatic on DSC-equipped radios)
When entering/leaving port or navigating in port waters
In busy waterways and during maneuvering

sections.prompt: What is the primary watchkeeping channel for distress and safety voice communications?
sections.explanation: Channel 16 is the international distress and safety frequency. All SOLAS vessels must maintain a continuous watch on Channel 16 while at sea.
